etymology.description: 爪(爪) (zhǎo, "hands") + 角(角) (jiǎo, "horn") as phonetic + 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") = combines hand and woman -> evolved into ideogrammic compound 母(母) (mǔ, "mother") + 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") + 女(女) (nǚ, "daughter")
etymology.interpretation: Initially, the character 婁(lóu) combined elements representing hands and a woman for an uncertain meaning. It later evolved to symbolically represent familial relationships through the components of mother and daughter.

examples.chinese: 他把木柴捆成一捆(他把木柴捆成一捆)，像一个大的婁(像一個大的婁)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ mùchái kǔn chéng yī kǔn, xiàng yī gè dà de lóu.
examples.english: He bundled the firewood into a bundle, like a big bundle.

examples.chinese: 这些棉花要编成婁(這些棉花要編成婁)，才方便运输(才方便運輸)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèxiē miánhuā yào biān chéng lóu, cái fāngbiàn yùnshū.
examples.english: These cottons need to be woven into bundles for easy transportation.

examples.chinese: 他手里拿着一个婁(他手裡拿著一個婁)，里面装满了干草(裡面裝滿了乾草)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shǒu lǐ názhe yī gè lóu, lǐmiàn zhuāngmǎnle gāncǎo.
examples.english: He held a bundle, filled with hay, in his hand.
